fullReview.reason: Two distractors encode the same reciprocal misconception: a small surface-area-to-volume ratio is equivalent to a large volume-to-surface-area ratio.
issues: The small-SA:V and large-V:SA distractors are mathematically equivalent, so the three distractors are not mutually distinct.

cueReview.reason: 🦠 represents the single-celled organism already explicit on the front. It adds no surface-area-to-volume information and remains neutral across every ratio choice.
